title: Market Brief — Tuesday, May 12, 2026
summary: GPU supply is bifurcated: A100s sit in oversupply at 85% availability while GB200s face severe shortage at 10%, with B200s scarce at 25% and H200s tight at 45%. H200 OCPI volatility hit 86.3% annualized, signaling frantic bidding on next-gen silicon as H100 and H200 utilization pin above 97%, the tightest compute market since the 2024 training surge.
paragraphs: GPU supply is bifurcated: A100s sit in oversupply at 85% availability while GB200s face severe shortage at 10%, with B200s scarce at 25% and H200s tight at 45%. H200 OCPI volatility hit 86.3% annualized, signaling frantic bidding on next-gen silicon as H100 and H200 utilization pin above 97%, the tightest compute market since the 2024 training surge.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), running Llama 70B on H100 costs roughly $9.84/M tokens, meaning DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.378/M output is selling at a loss of approximately $9.46 per million tokens. DeepSeek: Burning Cash. OpenAI o3 Pro at $80/M output prints 83-95% margins depending on tier: Strong. Anthropic Haiku 4.5 at $5/M output is Neutral, while AMD MI300X operators face $11.90/M TCO making mid-tier sales impossible: Weak.

B200 spot at $4.20/hr with only 25% availability is undervalued given 85.7% utilization surging 8.3pp in 30 days; accumulate B200 capacity now. OpenAI o3 Pro at $80/M output is overpriced relative to GPT-5.4 at $15/M on similar-class reasoning; short premium-tier inference exposure and rotate into B200 compute longs.
bullets: 10% availability on GB200s marks the most severe GPU shortage across all classes tracked.
25% B200 availability qualifies as scarce compute, yet utilization climbed 8.3 percentage points in 30 days.
$0.378 per million output tokens makes DeepSeek V3.2 the cheapest frontier-adjacent model, roughly 211x below o3 Pro.
99.6% H100 utilization is effectively sold out, up 12 percentage points month-over-month.
21.41 on the LLMflation Index means API pricing has fallen roughly 79% since GPT-4 launch baseline of 100.
86.3% annualized volatility on H200 OCPI is double A100 levels, reflecting acute supply-demand imbalance.
